title: Preset UX: expose preset selection in dispatcher
description: Add preset selection to the transient dispatcher. Apply @preset cookies via gptel--transform-apply-preset. Include ogent-flavored presets registered via gptel-make-preset.

title: Make ogent usable from any buffer with popup context
description:
**Vision**: Make ogent a true Emacs-wide AI assistant that can be invoked from any buffer while maintaining the structured, auditable conversation history that Org-mode provides.

**Current State**: ogent is tightly coupled to org-mode buffers, limiting its usefulness when working in code files, text buffers, or other major modes.

**Goal**: Implement the "Buffer Linking Outside Org-mode" architecture (specs/architecture.org:40-45):
- Invoking ogent from non-Org major mode establishes/reuses a companion Org buffer for the request/response transcript
- Default: create temp Org buffer named `*ogent:<file>*` with link stored in buffer-local metadata
- Users can override companion by selecting an existing Org buffer (prompt or config)
- Context reads from originating buffer, writes/streams to Org companion
- Editing actions respect active context: code buffers get inline diffs/patches, Org companion records prompts/completions
- Linking metadata survives buffer reloads via buffer-local vars in file-local settings
- Expose command to rebind companion Org buffer for workflow switches

**Why This Matters**: This aligns with ogent's core principle of keeping state local to buffers (architecture.org:11) while extending the minor mode's utility beyond just Org files, making ogent available wherever you're working in Emacs.

title: Make ogent commands globally available
description: Update keybindings and command definitions to work from any major mode, not just org-mode. Maintain ogent-mode's ownership of C-c o prefix per architecture.org:9. Ensure interactive entrypoints are autoloaded.

title: Tool + reasoning blocks: integrate gptel-highlight-mode
description: Reuse gptel-highlight-mode and block types (#+begin_tool, #+begin_reasoning) so tool calls and reasoning streams appear inline with folding support.

title: Docs: user-facing guide for gptel integration
description: Add a user-facing guide under docs/ covering API keys, backend customization via ogent-models.el, and how to define organization-specific presets.

title: Cancellation/retries: add abort and retry commands
description: Provide commands that look up request IDs in ogent-ui--request-table and call the appropriate gptel abort helper. Allow retrying failed requests.

title: Route responses to companion Org buffer
description: Ensure all streamed responses, prompts, and completions write to the companion Org buffer. Streaming callbacks must append to blocks in the companion buffer per architecture.org:34-38.

title: Handle inline edits in source buffers
description: When editing actions are requested from code buffers, apply inline diffs/patches to the source buffer while logging the prompts and completions to the Org companion. Keep the two buffers synchronized via markers for navigation commands.

title: Add command to rebind companion buffer
description: Implement interactive command allowing users to select/change the companion Org buffer. Users should be able to override the default `*ogent:<file>*` companion by selecting an existing Org buffer via prompt or config.

title: Implement ogent-onboard command for provider setup
description: Create an interactive onboarding command that guides users through setting up their first model provider (starting with Anthropic). Should handle API key configuration, backend setup, and verification.

title: Fix onboard verification and add recompile command
description: Backend verification fails because gptel-anthropic needs to be created with gptel-make-anthropic. Also need current model list and interactive recompile.

title: Implement buffer-local metadata linking
description: Store bidirectional link between source buffer and companion Org buffer in buffer-local variables. Ensure metadata survives buffer reloads via file-local settings. Keep state local to buffers per architecture.org:11.

title: Route context reads to originating buffer
description: Modify ogent-context-build to read context from the originating buffer (source code file) when invoked from non-Org mode, while maintaining current behavior for Org buffers. Follow Context Pipeline architecture.org:13-17.

title: Detect buffer type and create/reuse companion Org buffer
description: Check if current buffer is org-mode. If not, create or reuse a companion Org buffer named `*ogent:<file>*` for the request/response transcript. Follow the pattern specified in specs/architecture.org:40-45.

title: FSM + status UI: show gptel progress in block metadata
description: Bubble up gptel-send--handlers signals (WAIT/TYPE/DONE/ERRS) into header-line or block metadata so users can see progress and latency per model.
